description: The provided image displays a 'Check for updates' interface badge rather than experimental scientific data, rendering visual microstructural details unreadable. However, based on the comprehensive text extraction context provided for this Figure from the paper on Fe-28Mn-9Al-1.2C-Cr austenitic low-density steel, the intended content likely presents transmission electron microscopy characterization. The figure presumably illustrates the microstructural evolution across alloys with varying Cr content (0Cr, 3Cr, 6Cr, 9Cr wt%) and processing conditions (as-cast versus annealed at 550°C for various durations). Key features caption states include nano-scale κ-carbides (8-14 nm diameter) coherent with the FCC austenite matrix, DO3 ordered precipitates within BCC ferrite phases, dislocation density variations from high (as-cast) to low (annealed), and Cr23C6 carbides (100-200 nm) at grain boundaries in 9Cr samples. These observations correlate with mechanical performance metrics including yield strength (617-882 MPa) and elongation (20-44%).
